topicData.topic: tcs and anthropic , new deal for giving associates AI access
topicData.angle: tcs and anthropic , new deal for giving associates AI access

postText:
@TCS just signed a deal with @Anthropic to give its associates access to Claude.

50,000 employees. One AI rollout. That is a procurement headline, not a transformation story.

Here is what actually matters: giving people access to a tool and changing how they work are two completely different problems. Most enterprise AI deployments stall right there, in the gap between login credentials and actual behaviour change.

The question nobody is asking is what the workflow looks like on day 31, not day 1. Who decides which tasks get handed to Claude? Who checks the output? What happens when a client deliverable goes out with a confident AI error baked in?

Access is the easy part. The hard part is figuring out who owns the mistake. Most firms skip that conversation because it does not show up in a press release.

TCS is big enough to get this right and big enough to make a very visible mess of it.

Has your delivery manager been told who owns an error when Claude is in the loop on a client document?

#AI #BuildingWithAI #TechTakes
